reasoning: The question resolves YES if Jerome Powell is no longer Fed Chair before January 1, 2026. His term as Fed Chair expires in May 2026. President Trump and his allies have launched an unprecedented pressure campaign to oust him, including floating a 'shadow chair' idea and exploring firing him 'for cause' over a building renovation. However, legal obstacles exist (the Supreme Court upheld 'for cause' protections in May 2025), and Trump's advisors have cautioned that firing Powell would tank the financial markets. Powell himself has repeatedly vowed not to resign and aims to protect the institution's independence. While Fed Governor Adrian Kugler resigned early on August 1, 2025, a Polymarket contract on August 1 tracking whether Powell will be out by the end of 2025 was trading at just 12%, down significantly from earlier spikes. Given Powell's resolve and the risks to the market, it is unlikely Trump will fire him before January 2026, so a 12% probability is appropriate.
